title: Replace Geolocation Plugin with IP Registry Country Code Lookup
description:
## Context
The existing WordPress geolocation plugin was expensive, clunky, and a potential GDPR violation because it provided granular (non-country-level) data. The goal was to replace it with a lightweight country-code-only lookup.

## Implementation
1. Remove the existing geolocation plugin entirely.
2. Use the IP Registry API or client-side JavaScript IP-based geolocation to resolve the user's country code (ISO 2-letter code).
3. If country code is unavailable, default to "ZZ" (unknown).
4. Auto-populate Gravity Form field ID 39 with the resolved country code.
5. Gravity Forms' built-in conditional redirect logic then routes the user to the correct success page based on country.

## Future Consideration
A Salesforce lookup REST function could be added to check if the user already exists in the system, potentially packaged as a consulting service plugin.
acceptance_criteria: Old geolocation plugin is fully removed
Country code is resolved via IP Registry or client-side JS geolocation
Gravity Form field 39 is auto-populated with the country code
Form redirect logic works based on the populated country code
middleware_changes: WordPress footer script added to resolve user IP and populate Gravity Forms field 39 with the country code.
api_changes: Integration with IP Registry API (or equivalent client-side IP geolocation) for country code resolution.
implementation_notes: Developer used client-side JavaScript IP geolocation (navigator or IP API) to resolve the country code and auto-populate the Gravity Forms hidden field. Implemented via wp_footer action hook. GDPR-compliant since only country-level data is captured.
comment_summary: Developer implemented a JS-based solution that auto-populates form field 39 with the country code resolved from client IP.
